item.title: JSON parse missing try-catch in cloud/ state loaders
item.intent: No corrupted cloud state file causes an unhandled exception. All cloud state loaders recover gracefully with logged warnings and safe fallbacks.
item.description: Four cloud module state loaders use bare JSON.parse without try-catch: src/cloud/fleet/discovery.ts:43 (readFleetRegistry), src/cloud/trust-modes/switch.ts:44 (readTrustModeState), src/cloud/commands/queue.ts:46 (readQueueState), src/cloud/heartbeat/reporter.ts:39 (loadHeartbeatState). If any of these JSON files is corrupted (partial write, disk error, manual edit), the function throws an unhandled exception that propagates through the entire command, crashing with no useful error message. The same pattern was fixed in evolve/tools/manifest.ts and evolve/approval/queue.ts (BUG-012 sweep) but cloud/ was missed. Steps: (1) wrap JSON.parse in try-catch with console.warn + fallback return in each of the 4 functions; (2) fallback values: fleet={version:1,agents:[]}, trust-mode={version:1,mode:'paranoid',connected:false,changedAt:...}, queue={version:1,pending:[],history:[]}, heartbeat=null-or-default-state; (3) run cloud/ tests to confirm no regression. Scope: 4 one-liner additions across 4 files.
